findings.description: The entire explicit layout/geometry spec (container inset, box padding, text alignment, badge placement, W/2 math) has no corresponding rule in the candidate set — only the email-fallback rule exists for this whole blob. This is a severe under-clustering; a distinct 'icon callout layout & geometry' rule (hard_constraint/must) is missing entirely.

payload.description: Box inset by W/2, badge center exactly on box left edge, text never slides under badge, vertical centering formulas.
payload.name: icon_callout_layout_geometry

findings.description: Corner-radius spec is explicitly 'mandatory, never change' with an explicit forbid list (never simplify to uniform radius, never swap corners, never flip diagonal). No rule captures this hard forbid constraint; it is missing from the candidate group entirely.

payload.description: 5:1 asymmetric radii (40/8/40/8 box, 20/4/20/4 badge); never uniform-radius, never swap corners, never flip diagonal.
payload.name: corner_radius_mandatory

findings.description: The drop shadow requirement ('mandatory... required on every instance; without it the badge looks flat and pasted-on') is a distinct hard must-have rule not represented anywhere in the candidate group.

payload.description: White icon badge must carry drop shadow e.g. 0 4px 10px rgba(1,30,69,0.18) on every instance.
payload.name: icon_badge_drop_shadow

findings.description: Palette reference table mapping tokens (Brand Blue, Light Blue, White, Dark Navy) to specific hex/usage in this callout is not represented as a rule; color-token governance is entirely absent from the group.

payload.description: Brand Blue #00529b text/stroke, Light Blue #E6F0F9 box fill, White #ffffff badge fill, Dark Navy #011E45 shadow color.
payload.name: icon_callout_palette

findings.description: The scaling note defines a proportional relationship rule (inset = W/2, box left-padding = W/2+24px, badge margin-top = -H/2) that must hold if badge size changes — a distinct maintainable rule, currently unrepresented.

payload.description: If badge size changes from default 80px, preserve inset=W/2, padding=W/2+24px, margin-top=-H/2 relationships.
payload.name: icon_callout_scaling_relationships

findings.description: The email fallback rule lacks governance facets present in source: client scope (Outlook desktop/Word engine as the failing target vs Apple Mail/iOS/web as honoring clients), a preferred remediation (pre-composite PNG / baked background image) versus an acceptable degrade condition ('when not feasible'). Without these facets the rule cannot express conditional precedence between the strong recommended fix and the fallback acceptance.

conditions.fallback_action: ship live absolute-position version and accept Outlook degrade
conditions.preferred_action: pre-composite badge as transparent PNG or bake into background image

findings.description: The MJML code block is the canonical implementation reference for the layout/geometry/shadow rules, not specifically for the email-fallback rule alone. As currently the sole rule in this group, the fallback rule risks being (mis)linked as if it governs the whole snippet; relation to a layout/geometry rule (once added) should be established instead.

Source text frames the pre-composite/bake option as the 'brand-safe' path for pixel-perfect fidelity, implying a stronger polarity (should/must-attempt) than the degrade-acceptance clause; collapsing both into one rule with a single hardness='strong_default'/polarity='should' loses the distinction between the recommended remediation and the tolerated exception.
